 {"id":"br-1t0e","title":"Resources: file_reservations resource should be bounded (avoid glob explosion)","description":"## Problem
`resource://file_reservations/{slug}` currently expands glob patterns via filesystem enumeration and may run `git log` over many pathspecs. On large projects with broad patterns (e.g. `src/**`), this can take >60s and effectively wedges the MCP server (subsequent tool/resource calls time out).

## Objective
Make `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` reliably fast and non-blocking for large repos while keeping legacy output shape.

## Scope
- Avoid enumerating all filesystem matches for glob patterns.
- Compute git activity without expanding to thousands of explicit paths (use git pathspec/glob or prefix-based strategy).
- Bound work per pattern (time/size caps) and fail open by returning `null` activity timestamps when exceeding caps.
- Ensure cleanup logic (TTL expiry + stale release) cannot monopolize the server request thread.

## Tests
- Unit tests covering glob-heavy patterns (`src/**`, `crates/**`, `**/*.rs`) ensure the code path does not enumerate full repo trees (use a temp dir with many files to assert completes quickly).
- Regression test: resource handler completes within a small time budget under a synthetic repo with many files and a broad glob reservation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resource://file_reservations/...` returns within a bounded time on large repos (no tool server wedge).
2. Output shape remains compatible (fields present, timestamps may be null when capped).
3. Existing conformance tests still pass; add targeted tests for the new caps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:05.771837055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745259737Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:29:19.745241343Z","close_reason":"Avoid glob explosion in resource://file_reservations: stop filesystem glob expansion; use git pathspec glob magic; add regression test; ran fmt+clippy+test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1t0e","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:13:34.586726901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1uf","title":"Share/Export Pipeline Parity (Static Bundle)","description":"## Objective
Implement the full **Share/Export pipeline** (static bundle) as specified in `EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md` (Share/Export section). This includes DB snapshotting, scrub presets, search indexes, viewer assets, bundle scaffolding, signing/encryption, and deterministic ZIP packaging.

## Scope (15 Steps)
1. create_sqlite_snapshot
2. apply_project_scope
3. scrub_snapshot (presets + secret regex)
4. build_search_indexes (FTS5)
5. build_materialized_views
6. create_performance_indexes
7. finalize_snapshot_for_export
8. bundle_attachments
9. maybe_chunk_database
10. copy_viewer_assets
11. export_viewer_data
12. write_bundle_scaffolding
13. sign_manifest
14. encrypt_bundle
15. package_directory_as_zip

## Tests
- Unit tests per step for deterministic behavior.
- Integration tests for end‑to‑end export/update flows.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.4`.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Share pipeline artifacts under `tests/artifacts/share/<timestamp>/`.
- JSON summaries + diffs for each step.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All steps are implemented per spec with legacy parity.
2. Deterministic bundles (stable hashes) are enforced in tests.
3. +{"id":"br-21gj","title":"Dual-mode interface program: MCP-first default + opt-in agent-first CLI","description":"Background:
Users asked for an explicit dual-mode interface strategy:
1) MCP mode must remain the default.
2) A separate setting (OFF by default) can disable MCP-first behavior and enable a full agent-first CLI command surface.
3) When MCP mode is ON, CLI invocation should fail fast with a clear message like "Agent Mail is not a CLI!" and actionable guidance.

Why this matters:
The current surface area is confusing across binaries and command families. We need one coherent contract so operators, agents, and docs all align.

Outcome:
A production-ready dual-mode architecture with explicit guardrails, clear UX, test coverage, and rollout playbook.

Program-level definition of done:
- Mode contract is documented and enforced.
- MCP default remains unchanged.
- CLI mode is explicit opt-in.
- Denial path in MCP mode is deterministic and tested.
- CLI mode command parity roadmap is implemented and validated.","design":"Dual-mode interface is implemented as a single authoritative mode decision (resolver) consumed by all entrypoints. Mode controls command dispatch policy, UX messaging, and allowed command families. MCP mode remains default and is conservative by design; CLI mode is explicit opt-in via config switch.","acceptance_criteria":"1) MCP mode is default and denies CLI command execution with canonical message/hints. 2) CLI mode opt-in enables documented command families with agent-first ergonomics. 3) Behavior is validated by unit/integration/conformance/perf tests. 4) Docs and rollout playbooks are updated.","notes":"Planning assumptions: no implicit mode auto-switching; no hidden compatibility shims; no mode decisions duplicated in per-command handlers.  {"id":"br-hk3v","title":"Dev UX: am runner script + /api<->/mcp HTTP path alias","description":"## Problem
Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the alias path logic (path_allowed + canonicalization).

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/am serve works without manual exports in a typical dev setup (token file present).
- With default config (HTTP_PATH unset), both POST /api/ and POST /mcp/ reach the MCP handler.
- Full quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.
